ocr.text: The evaluation of functional abilities in various populations is critical for developing tailored interventions and enhancing
overall quality of life. In this context, the data presented in the accompanying table highlights several functional assessment
tools, including the Pfeffer Functional Activities Questionnaire, which is widely utilized for assessing daily living skills among
older adults.
Abstracts  Adaptations or
Functional assessment tools  citation*  normative data†
1. Pfeffer Functional Activities Questionnaire  4  NS ‡
2. Activities of Daily Living (ADL) + InstrumentalActivities of Daily Living (IADL)  3  47
3. Activities of Daily Living (ADL)  3  48
4. Hoehn & Yahr Staging  2  49
5. Schwab & England Scale (SES)  2  NS
6. Katz' Index of ADL  2  NS
7. Functional Assessment Staging (FAST)  2  NS
8. Bayer-Activities of Daily Living  2  NS
9. Unified Parkinson's Disease Rating Scale-Activitiesof Daily Living Section (UPDRS-ADL)  1  NS
10. Barthel Index  1  50
11. Activities of Daily Living - InternationalScale  1  NS
This tool provides valuable insights into an individual's capacity to perform essential activities and can serve as an important
metric for healthcare providers in both clinical and research settings. The table further details relevant citation information and
normative data, which serve as benchmarks for interpreting the results obtained from these assessments. The presence of
normative data is particularly crucial, as it allows practitioners to compare an individual's performance against established
standards, thereby facilitating more accurate assessments of functional impairment. By leveraging such comprehensive tools
and data, practitioners can better identify areas of need, monitor progress over time, and ultimately enhance the effectiveness of
interventions aimed at improving functional independence in at-risk populations.
